_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn how multiple ethical frameworks can guide decision-making and explore similarities and differences between standard frameworks and alternatives.

_source.w: We learn how multiple ethical frameworks can guide decision-making and explore similarities and differences between standard frameworks and alternatives.
y: Using multiple frameworks produces more comprehensive and defensible ethical reasoning.
Comparing frameworks reveals different assumptions about what matters most in ethical life.
Awareness of diverse ethical traditions, including non-Western approaches, enriches our moral thinking.
eg: We apply consequences, duties and virtues frameworks simultaneously to a complex ethical issue.
We investigate alternative ethical frameworks — Indigenous ethics, feminist ethics, care ethics.
We compare the assumptions underlying different frameworks and discuss their implications.
We evaluate which frameworks are most helpful for different types of ethical dilemmas.
We discuss how frameworks from different cultural traditions can complement each other.
We construct ethical arguments that draw on multiple frameworks to support their conclusions.
